message: The kong default run is still exposed as an explicit `run` subcommand, adding a command outside the approved CLI surface and changing task composition for tasks whose first unquoted word is `run`.
trigger: A user invokes the CLI with an unquoted task whose first positional word is exactly `run`, for example `debate run migrate` or `debate run`.
fix_direction: Model the default run path without exposing a selectable `run` command name, or otherwise route an explicit leading `run` token back into the positional task so the only supported run form remains bare `debate <task>`.
evidence: `Run runCmd` is registered as a kong command with `default:"withargs"`; kong default commands remain selectable by their command name. The contract allows only the bare default run plus `version`, `init`, and `new <name>`, and says adding new subcommands is out of scope. Before this change, `debate run migrate` fell through to run parsing and assembled the task as `run migrate`; now `run` is consumed as the command name and the task becomes `migrate`.

message: The new max-rounds before/after test can pass even if --max-rounds is ignored.
trigger: when --max-rounds parsing regresses or the flag is ignored in either before/after positional ordering
fix_direction: Assert the actual outcome and round count, for example by using --json and checking outcome="max" and rounds=1, or by checking participant prompt counts with a fixture that cannot stalemate before the expected max.
evidence: The fixture repeats the same not-done signal each round, and the only assertion is exit code 2. Production exitCode returns 2 for both "max" and "stalemate", so an ignored --max-rounds flag can still pass by reaching stalemate under the default max.

message: The tests do not exercise the top-level Kong CLI path that main actually uses.
trigger: when the full top-level parser handles default-run and subcommand coexistence differently from the reduced helper parsers
fix_direction: Add tests that call parseCLI directly for bare run flags before/after the task and for top-level new <name> --role synthesizer, using injected workDir and mock resolver.
evidence: main calls parseCLI, which wires the full cli struct containing the default run command plus version/init/new. The added tests call parseAndRun, which builds a reduced app with only Run, and cmdNew, which uses parseStandaloneCommand; there are no parseCLI references in cmd/debate/*_test.go.

message: Kong parse-error paths have no targeted regression tests.
trigger: when a user supplies an unknown flag or another argument form rejected by Kong before command Run executes
fix_direction: Add parseCLI or command-wrapper tests for representative unknown flags, asserting non-zero exit and useful usage/error text on stderr.
evidence: parseAndDispatch now has Kong parse-error handling, but cmd/debate tests only cover domain errors such as init extra args, invalid role, missing name, and unknown backend. No test covers an unknown CLI flag or malformed Kong parse path.

message: The change introduces duplicate Kong parsing entrypoints outside the production CLI path.
fix_direction: Keep one CLI parser entrypoint for argument parsing tests, preferably parseCLI; move command behavior into runDebate/runInit/runNew helpers that accept parsed structs, and remove the standalone parser wrappers.
evidence: main() calls parseCLI at cmd/debate/main.go:25, but parseAndRun builds a separate anonymous Kong app at cmd/debate/main.go:199-202, and cmdInit/cmdNew use parseStandaloneCommand at cmd/debate/scaffold.go:68-73 and 146-151. rg shows parseAndRun/cmdInit/cmdNew are called by tests, not the production entrypoint.

message: The CLI docs still say `debate new` flags must come before the name, so the newly supported after-name form is undocumented.
trigger: when users consult the CLI docs for where `debate new --role` may be placed after the kong migration
fix_direction: Update the CLI docs to remove the before-name-only note or explicitly state that `--role` works before or after the persona name.
evidence: docs/DESIGN.md documents `debate new [--role ...] <name>` with `(флаги ДО имени)`, while the updated tests assert `[]string{"after", "--role", "synthesizer"}` works in cmd/debate/scaffold_test.go:163.

message: The CLI docs advertise a `-t` short alias for `--task`, but the command only defines `--task`.
trigger: when a user follows the docs and runs `debate -t @file`
fix_direction: Either remove `-t` from the docs or add a supported `-t` short alias if that is intended.
evidence: docs/DESIGN.md says `-t/--task @file`; cmd/debate/main.go:74 defines `TaskFlag` with `name:"task"` and no `short:"t"` tag.

message: The docs list a `debate validate` subcommand that is not implemented.
trigger: when a user follows the documented subcommand list and runs `debate validate`
fix_direction: Remove `validate` from current CLI documentation or clearly mark it as a future/planned command.
evidence: docs/DESIGN.md lists `debate validate`; cmd/debate/main.go:52-57 defines only the default run command plus `version`, `init`, and `new`.
